calendar.matters:
CRC 3.1385 ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar Oct-20-2022 Off Calendar

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Award Of Attorneys Fees, Costs, & Expenses; And Memorandum Of Points & Authorities
Off Calendar Aug-11-2022 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

APPEARANCES (VIA ZOOM VIDEOCONFERENCE):
Atty. VANESSA V. DAO, Esq. of Clark Hill LLP (Tel: 619-557-0404) for defendant FCA US LLC;
No other appearances made in this matter.

HEARING RE: PLAINTIFF NICHOLAS SOWERS, NICHOLAS SOWERS, and MARISSA BERTRAM's Motion For Award Of Attorneys Fees, Costs, & Expenses.

Matter not contested/argued. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

Plaintiffs' motion for attorney fees, costs and expenses in this lemon law case is granted in the total amount of $47,153.
The parties settled, agreeing that defendants would pay plaintiffs' fees, costs and expenses "reasonably incurred." I reviewed plaintiffs' counsel's daily billing record and found the time (59 hours) and tasks reasonable. Based on my experience adjudicating some 20 lemon law cases and 38 years' exposure to attorney billing rates in the San Francisco Bay Area, I also find $600 an hour for the work to be reasonable. (See Serrano v. Priest (1977) 20 Cal.3d 25, 49.) The lodestar amount is thus $35,400.

A .3 enhancement is warranted as the case was on contingency and the hybrid vehicle raised new legal issues. The fee award is thus $46,020.

Costs and expenses of $1,133 are undisputed.

Order signed.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer Jr.; Clerk: Sean Kane; Not Reported. (302/RBU)

Off Calendar Jan-07-2022 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances via Zoom Conference
Michael R. Vachon, Esq., VACHON LAW FIRM, 858.674.4100, michael@vachonlaw.com, for Plaintiffs, Nicholas Sowers & Marissa Bertram,
Erin Hanson, Esq., CLARK HILL LLP, (619) 557-0404, EHanson@ClarkHill.com, for Defendant, FCA US LLC.
() Court reporter, CSR #.

Hearing is held. The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument, Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows: (as fully reported in the reporter's notes/transcript)

PLAINTIFFS NICHOLAS SOWERS, NICHOLAS SOWERS and MARISSA BERTRAM'S Motion To Compel Fca Us, Llc To Serve Further Responses To Requests For Admissions And Form Interrogatory No. 17.1.

The judge pro tem's report and recommendation is adopted. Plaintiffs' "motion to compel FCA US, LLC to serve further responses to requests for admissions and form interrogatory No. 17.1" is granted in part. FCA is ordered to serve code-compliant responses to RFAs 3-6, 8, 14, 17-20 and 32 and to form interrogatory 17.1. Sanctions of $2,584 are awarded to plaintiffs.

Order is signed.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer, Clerk: W. Trupek, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano CSR# 8600, email: maria.torreano@gmail.com, Reported. (302/RBU)
